summary: Burr's conjecture (every $(2k-2)$-chromatic digraph contains every oriented tree of order $k$) remains open in full generality. The strongest general upper bound has been improved from quadratic to subquadratic: Bessy, Gonçalves, and Reinald (2024) proved that every digraph with chromatic number $8\sqrt{2/15}\,k\sqrt{k}+O(k)$ contains any oriented tree of order $k$, the first subquadratic bound. A 2024 survey by Stein provides a comprehensive review of the state of the problem.

since_posted.title: Oriented trees in O(k√k)-chromatic digraphs, a subquadratic bound for Burr's conjecture
since_posted.authors: Stéphane Bessy, Daniel Gonçalves, Amadeus Reinald
since_posted.venue: Graph-Theoretic Concepts in Computer Science (WG 2024), Springer LNCS 15153
since_posted.claim: First subquadratic bound for Burr's conjecture: every digraph with chromatic number $8\sqrt{2/15}\,k\sqrt{k}+O(k)$ contains any oriented tree of order $k$; also gives improved bounds for arborescences ($\sqrt{4/3}\,k\sqrt{k}+O(k)$) and paths on $b$ blocks ($(b-1)(k-3)+3$).

since_posted.title: Oriented trees and paths in digraphs
since_posted.venue: Surveys in Combinatorics 2024, Cambridge University Press
since_posted.claim: Comprehensive survey of conditions (chromatic number, edge density, minimum out-degree, semidegree) guaranteeing the presence of all oriented paths or trees of given order in a digraph, including the status of Burr's conjecture and related open problems.

notes: The Springer chapter link (10.1007/978-3-031-75409-8_6) redirected to an auth wall and could not be fetched directly; venue confirmed from the arXiv abstract metadata. The 2025 Yuster paper (arXiv:2512.21950) on acyclic subgraphs of high-chromatic digraphs is tangentially related but does not directly advance Burr's conjecture. The conjecture remains fully open at the stated bound of $2k-2$.
